reviews.review_msg: Attend lectures! Content is difficult to understand without examples. Quizzes were pretty difficult with some trick questions-especially with how it's worded. She genuinely cares about the topic and our learning. Very sweet and accommodating. Topics are super interesting. Her outfits never miss!

Highly recommend taking PSYC221 first and saving your science credit for this, the class is a breeze with the prior knowledge from 221- so much overlap. Her slides were strangely formatted and all over the place regarding content. She was also impossible to reach, my friends and I probably sent a total of 15 emails and never got anything back.

• Took LING315 with Meg and I just love her, so I chose to take LING100. She really loves psycholinguistics—so anything to do with that is her bread and butter.
• Course was really easy since I had already taken many of the 300-level linguistics courses.
• Lots of homework & papers, beware!

Prof Grant is very approachable and does her best to make the material accessible. She also uses recorded lectures which is a plus but I personally found the material challenging and struggled to grasp some of the concepts. If you're new to linguistics, be prepared to put in extra effort, as the content can be difficult to understand at times.

I loved Dr. Grant's class; I found Ling 100 to be very interesting. Assignments were fairly easy and her exams were also fair and based mostly on her slides and some extra online external content posted online (since this class was blended offline/online). Her slides can be confusing but she does elaborate on her slides in lectures which helped me

The material she presents in lectures is hard to piece together since she makes them feel like different topics rather than ideas that intertwine. Lectures don't make any connections between topics and ideas, is not clear, and is not engaging. Only good thing is content is easy and you don't need to attend lectures at all.
She is a good professor with interesting lectures. The assignments reflect what u learn in the class and the midterm isn't too hard. good class

I struggled with the course content because I was talking Cogs 100 as B-Sci course. Dr. Grant's teaching style didn't necessarily match my personal learning style therefore I struggled in the class, but she was suuuper helpful when I attended her office hours and did everything to help as I didn't feel confident that I would pass (I did).

Very good prof. Clearly passionate in Linguistics. Lectures are very engaging and she gives good examples from the theory provided. The readings are mostly based on the lectures too, but it helps to just grasp information in two different formats.

Dr Grant is so nice but her lectures feel very disorienting. The material she presents in lectures is hard to piece together since she makes them feel like different topics rather than ideas that intertwine (lectures don't make the connections between topics and ideas clear nor engaging).

Textbook online, and she tells you what parts to read. Pretty engaging lectures, and tutorials are a must go-to, since it is during tutorials that you learn how to do most of the concepts. Definitely ask questions in tutorial, she is super caring and will explain any question you have, no matter what it is. Great experience.

Accidentally took this class in my very first semester. Pretty easy. Loved her lectures. She explained things in a simple manner. Instructions for assignments were clear. Tutorials were extremely helpful. Be sure to read the textbook. Tests ranged between easy and average. Didn't have a midterm or final exam.

Skipped 70% of lectures and did fine. Her slides aren't the best, had to find other sources to learn how to do certain things, which was fairly easy. Do your assignments and make sure you know the material for the exam, and they're quite simple and easy to do well on. TA's were quite helpful too.
